input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows.

---

## 1. Install the .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer (latest .NET 8 or 9)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to discover the exact name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install it
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

The Windows (WinUI 3) target needs the Windows App SDK, which comes with the VS workload:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or, if you already have VS, add the required workloads via CLI:
# Discover available workload IDs
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  export --channelId VisualStudio.17.Release

# Install the MAUI and UWP/WinUI workloads
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --quiet --norestart
```

## 4. Install Android SDK & Emulator

The MAUI workload bundles Android pieces, but you can manage them explicitly:

```powershell
# Check what Android components the workload installed
dotnet workload list

# If you need the standalone SDK (e.g. for CI), use sdkmanager:
# Default path after workload install:
$sdkManager = "$env:USERPROFILE\.dotnet\packs\Microsoft.Android.Sdk.Windows\*\tools\sdkmanager.bat"
# Discover the actual resolved path
Resolve-Path $sdkManager

# List available packages dynamically
& (Resolve-Path $sdkManager) --list

# Install specific components (API level, build tools, emulator)
& (Resolve-Path $sdkManager) `
  "platform-tools" `
  "platforms;android-35" `
  "build-tools;35.0.0" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
  "emulator"

# Accept all licenses
& (Resolve-Path $sdkManager) --licenses
```

## 5. Set Environment Variables

```powershell
# Point to the Android SDK managed by the workload
$androidSdk = "$env:USERPROFILE\.dotnet\packs\Microsoft.Android.Sdk.Windows"
$latestVersion = (Get-ChildItem $androidSdk | Sort-Object Name -Descending | Select-Object -First 1).FullName

[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", "$latestVersion\tools", "User")
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_SDK_ROOT", "$latestVersion\tools", "User")

# Refresh current session
$env:ANDROID_HOME = "$latestVersion\tools"
```

## 6. Create an Android Emulator

```powershell
$avdManager = Resolve-Path "$latestVersion\tools\avdmanager.bat"

# List available device definitions
& $avdManager list device

# Create an AVD
& $avdManager create avd `
  --name "Pixel_MAUI" `
  --package "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
  --device "pixel_7"
```

## 7. Enable Windows Developer Mode (for WinUI target)

```powershell
# Required for sideloading the WinUI 3 app
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense" /d 1
```

## 8. Scaffold & Build a Test Project

```powershell
# List available MAUI templates
dotnet new list maui

# Create a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run

# Run on Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0 -t:Run
```

## 9. Discover Target Framework Versions Dynamically

```powershell
# See which TFMs a project supports
Select-String -Path HelloMaui.csproj -Pattern "TargetFrameworks"

# Discover installed Android API levels
dotnet --info | Select-String -Pattern "android"

# List all installed .NET SDKs and runtimes
dotnet --info
```

## 10. Verify Everything

```powershell
# One-liner health check (available in .NET 9+)
dotnet workload list
dotnet new maui --dry-run -n VerifySetup
```

---

**Key notes:**
- `winget` ships with Windows 11 by default.
- Replace `net9.0` with `net8.0` if you install .NET 8 instead.
- The `Resolve-Path` + wildcard pattern is how you dynamically find versioned SDK paths without hardcoding.
- Run PowerShell as **Administrator** for steps 3 and 7.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They want PowerShell commands and dynamic version discovery. Let me provide a comprehensive plan.
